filesCreated.purpose: Editor component - fetches and displays post data in editor
Frontend template - renders post data on frontend
Block icon (post document with hero section)

warnings: Asset size limit warnings (editor.js 337 KiB) - expected for theme bundles, not critical

syncStatus.notes: TSX and PHP structure verified to match. Both use same conditional logic for topic pill, excerpt, and all content sections. Topic accent color applied to both section element (when useTopicColor is true) and pill element (always) in both TSX and PHP.

postSetup.title: TEST: Post Hero
postSetup.excerpt: Navigating university relationships can be challenging. Learn practical strategies for building meaningful connections with both professors and fellow students that will enhance your academic experience.
postSetup.author: leandro

implementationNotes.dataSourcePattern: Block uses useSelect in TSX to fetch current post data via @wordpress/editor getCurrentPostId() and @wordpress/core-data getEntityRecord(). PHP uses standard WordPress functions: get_the_title(), get_post_thumbnail_id(), get_the_category(), get_the_author(), get_the_date(), get_the_excerpt().
implementationNotes.accentColorMechanism: Page accent color applied to body element by existing filter. When useTopicColor is true, the topic's accent_color class is added to the section element to override gradient CSS vars. The pill ALWAYS uses the topic's accent color (independent of useTopicColor setting) by applying the accent_color class to the pill span element.
implementationNotes.pillPaddingAdjustment: Used px-2 py-2 (8px all around) for pill padding per planning spec, matching Figma exactly.
implementationNotes.editorBehavior: Block uses renameBlock() with useRef/useEffect pattern (same as FeaturedPost) to sync block name with post title. In template editing context without a post, shows placeholder content.
implementationNotes.emptyStateHandling: Topic pill only renders when category exists. Excerpt only renders when showExcerpt is true AND post has excerpt. Background image only renders when featured image exists. All other fields (title, author, date) always render with fallback placeholders in editor.
implementationNotes.noThemeHeading: Did not use ThemeHeading component because all fields are dynamic from post data (not editable). Custom h1 element used instead of RichText component.
implementationNotes.heroSecondaryPattern: Followed Hero secondary variation structure exactly: section.hero > div.top-gradient + div.hero-inner > background image layer + content layer. Used same mask-image gradient, same padding calculations, same container structure.
